nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Pinned Python dependency lockfile autogenerated by uv from pyproject.toml (rich and its transitive dependencies).

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Required setup orchestrator that runs the per-module setup scripts (bash, zsh, git, claude) to configure the shell environment and symlinks.
nodes.languageNotes: Canonicalizes to the main worktree before creating symlinks to avoid baking linked-worktree paths into ~/.claude-* symlinks (issue #589).

nodes.name: create_symlink
nodes.summary: Creates a symlink from a source dotfile to its target location, handling existing files and reporting via the UX library.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Agent-facing guide for the aws/ module, documenting its single responsibility, SSOT principles, execution flow, settings.json merge policy, and external-PC safety gate.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step bootstrap guide for internal-PC AWS Bedrock + Claude Code setup, covering setup.sh, aws.local.sh edits, SSO login, OTel telemetry install, diagnosis, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: diagnose.sh
nodes.summary: Read-only diagnostic script that verifies the internal-PC Claude Code / AWS Bedrock bootstrap, reporting PASS/FAIL/WARN for setup.sh and OTel install results without modifying any files.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps its body in a subshell with set -uo pipefail so copy-paste execution stays safe.

nodes.name: install-otel-managed-settings.sh
nodes.summary: Idempotent installer that writes /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json with the internal OTLP telemetry endpoint and a dynamic user.id derived from the AWS caller identity.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Internal-PC AWS Bedrock + Claude Code bootstrap that seeds aws.local.sh and ~/.aws/config and jq-deep-merges the dotfiles settings SSOT with a Bedrock overlay into a real ~/.claude/settings.json; a no-op on external/public PCs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses /bin/bash (not sh) to source ux_lib safely and gates all work behind a setup-mode check.

nodes.name: _aws_setup_mode
nodes.summary: Reads and canonicalizes the ~/.dotfiles-setup-mode value so the bootstrap can gate itself to internal PCs only.

nodes.name: _seed_file
nodes.summary: Idempotently seeds a target config file from a template only when it does not already exist, preserving prior user edits.

nodes.name: _merge_claude_settings_json
nodes.summary: Deep-merges the dotfiles Claude settings SSOT with the Bedrock overlay via jq into a real ~/.claude/settings.json, working around a settings.local.json .env merge failure on internal PCs.

nodes.name: _archive_legacy_settings_local
nodes.summary: Archives any legacy settings.local.json so it no longer shadows the freshly merged real settings.json.

nodes.name: _env_registered
nodes.summary: Helper that checks whether a given environment variable is registered/exported, used by the diagnostic checks.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Agent-context guide for the claude/ module explaining the skills SSOT, per-tool connection scheme (issue #791), config-file symlink-vs-copy rationale, and plugin manifest layout.
nodes.languageNotes: Bilingual (Korean/English) internal maintainer documentation.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Short global instructions doc defining the Advisor/Worker model role split for delegating open-ended implementation to opus subagents.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: User-facing setup guide for the Claude Code configuration module covering first-time setup, internal-PC env block, shared-settings customization, and SKILL.md writing rules.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code settings SSOT declaring env vars, session hooks, the statusline command, enabled plugins, TUI theme, and telemetry options.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Installer that symlinks skills/docs and copies settings.json into each Claude config account, handling legacy-path migrations, sandbox checks, and single- vs multi-account linking.
nodes.languageNotes: POSIX shell installer with several idempotent legacy-migration helpers.

nodes.name: statusline-command.sh
nodes.summary: Renders the Claude Code status line from session JSON on stdin, formatting model, token usage, and context info.

nodes.name: _migrate_legacy_statusline_command
nodes.summary: Rewrites any legacy statusLine command path in an account's settings.json to the current symlinked location.

nodes.name: _migrate_legacy_plugin_paths
nodes.summary: Migrates outdated plugin cache paths in account configuration to the current plugin layout.

nodes.name: _migrate_install_gh_issue_flow_stop_hook
nodes.summary: Installs or migrates the gh-issue-flow stop hook entry into an account's settings.json hooks block.

nodes.name: _print_stale_bind_mount_sudoers_hint
nodes.summary: Prints a remediation hint when stale sandbox bind-mount sudoers entries are detected.

nodes.name: _single_account_ensure_link
nodes.summary: Ensures the correct symlink exists for a single-account Claude config directory, replacing stale links as needed.

nodes.name: plugin-sync-session.sh
nodes.summary: SessionStart/Stop hook that closes the entry-path gap for /plugin slash commands: snapshots the plugin SSOT files at session start and, on Stop, diffs them to drive plugin-sync.sh's add/remove branches when they changed.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sha256sum/shasum fallback for macOS and jq set-difference (keys $a - $b) to detect marketplace/plugin removals.

nodes.name: plugin-sync.sh
nodes.summary: PostToolUse:Bash hook for `claude plugin ...` commands that merges the ground-truth plugin SSOT into the tracked public/private manifest files and auto-commits (and pushes on protected branches) any resulting change.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy jq transforms for union-merge/removal; commits with ALLOW_MAIN_COMMIT=1 to bypass the protected-branch guard for automated manifest syncs.

nodes.name: post-bash-dispatch.sh
nodes.summary: Single front-door PostToolUse:Bash dispatcher that parses the event envelope once and routes to post-gh-pr-create.sh or plugin-sync.sh only when the command cheaply looks relevant, halving process spawns on the common non-matching path.

nodes.name: post-gh-pr-create.sh
nodes.summary: PostToolUse:Bash hook for `gh pr create` that extracts the new PR number and syncs the GitHub project board (PR to In review, linked issues to In progress), polling to absorb the projectV2 auto-add race.
nodes.languageNotes: Derives the URL-match host from _dotfiles_setup_mode / GH_HOST and uses a bounded retry loop to survive GitHub's late auto-add of the PR card.

nodes.name: session-start-pc-context.sh
nodes.summary: SessionStart hook that injects the current PC's dotfiles setup-mode (public/internal/external) plus hostname into the session as additionalContext, so a fresh session knows its per-machine mode.

nodes.name: session-start-plugin-path-normalize.sh
nodes.summary: SessionStart hook that re-stamps plugin installLocation/installPath fields in the shared plugin SSOT to the active $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR spelling, so Claude Code's literal prefix validation passes for every multi-account symlinked store.
nodes.languageNotes: Idempotent in-place rewrite via a shared jq renorm() def; compares reserialized output to avoid churning mtimes and the sibling hook's baseline hash.

nodes.name: session-start-settings-drift.sh
nodes.summary: SessionStart hook that warns when the live settings.json `.hooks` block has drifted from the dotfiles SSOT (claude/settings.json), pointing the user at the correct re-seed entry point for their mode.

nodes.name: skill_step_catalog.yml
nodes.summary: SSOT catalog for skill_completion_guard.py declaring which multi-step SKILL.md files have step-skip protection and the ordered step IDs each must emit before the model may end its turn.

nodes.name: _snapshot
nodes.summary: Snapshots the plugin SSOT as {mp_hash, pl_hash, mp_keys, pl_keys}, using a sha256sum/shasum fallback so change detection still works on macOS.

nodes.name: _extract_target
nodes.summary: Awk helper that returns the first non-flag argument following a keyword token, so flags placed between a subcommand and its target are not mistaken for the target.

nodes.name: _commit_if_changed
nodes.summary: Stages the given manifest files, commits only when there is a staged diff (using ALLOW_MAIN_COMMIT=1 to bypass the protected-branch guard), and unstages cleanly on commit failure before pushing protected branches.

nodes.name: _push_if_protected
nodes.summary: Pushes the just-committed branch only when it is a protected branch (main/master) with a configured upstream, keeping local main from diverging after an auto-commit.

nodes.name: _post_gh_pr_create_repo_has_board
nodes.summary: Returns success when GH_REPO has at least one projectV2 board via a GraphQL count query, so boardless repos skip the retry budget entirely.

nodes.name: _normalize_file
nodes.summary: Rewrites one JSON file in place with the given jq program, touching it (with a single overwrite backup) only when normalization changes semantic content, comparing against a reserialized original for idempotence.

nodes.name: marketplaces.json
nodes.summary: Public plugin-marketplace manifest mapping marketplace names to their GitHub repos; serves as the SSOT that reconcile.sh and restore.sh compare against and install from.

nodes.name: plugins.json
nodes.summary: Public manifest listing the installed Claude Code plugins per marketplace, kept in sync incrementally by the plugin-sync hook and fully recomputed by reconcile.sh.

nodes.name: publish-sync.sh
nodes.summary: Publishes locally-committed plugin-manifest changes that branch protection forbids pushing directly, building the publish commit with git plumbing against origin/main, opening and merging a PR, then reconciling local main.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses git plumbing (hash-object, read-tree scratch index, write-tree, commit-tree) to build commits without touching HEAD or the working index; relies on patch-id detection to drop redundant sync commits during rebase.

nodes.name: reconcile.sh
nodes.summary: Full-recompute drift detector and repair tool that rebuilds the dotfiles plugin manifests to exactly match the ~/.claude-shared SSOT, pruning ghost entries the incremental sync hook can never remove. Supports --check and --apply.

nodes.name: restore.sh
nodes.summary: Reinstalls Claude Code plugins and marketplaces from the dotfiles manifest into the correct account-routed config dir(s); add-only by default, with --sync to also prune local items absent from the SSOT.
nodes.languageNotes: Resolves account-routed CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR values (multi-account mode) and injects them before every `claude plugin` call to avoid installing into the wrong config directory.

nodes.name: _repo_target
nodes.summary: Resolves the target repo (owner/name and remote) for the publish operation.

nodes.name: _fetch_origin
nodes.summary: Fetches origin/main so the publish commit is built against the latest remote state.

nodes.name: _build_publish_commit
nodes.summary: Constructs a manifest-sync commit against origin/main using git plumbing (hash-object, scratch-index read-tree, write-tree, commit-tree) without moving HEAD or touching the real index.

nodes.name: _publish_branch
nodes.summary: Pushes the freshly built commit as a new publish branch ref on the remote.

nodes.name: _open_and_merge_pr
nodes.summary: Opens a pull request for the publish branch and merges it, satisfying branch-protection rules that forbid a direct push of manifest changes.

nodes.name: _publish_manifest_diff
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full publish flow for a manifest diff: build commit, push branch, open and merge PR.

nodes.name: _cleanup_local_main_if_pure_sync
nodes.summary: Advances local main to the published origin/main — fast-forwarding or rebasing — but only when every ahead-commit is a pure sync-manifest commit and the working tree is clean, using patch-id detection to drop redundant commits.

nodes.name: _public_publish_allowed
nodes.summary: Gate check determining whether publishing to the public repo is permitted in the current environment.

nodes.name: _read_json_or
nodes.summary: Reads a JSON file, returning a fallback default when the file is missing or unreadable.

nodes.name: _diff_marketplaces
nodes.summary: Computes the drift between the SSOT and the dotfiles marketplaces manifest.

nodes.name: _diff_plugins
nodes.summary: Computes the drift between the SSOT and the dotfiles plugins manifest.

nodes.name: _run_check
nodes.summary: Default --check mode: prints the SSOT-vs-manifest diff and exits non-zero when drift is detected.

nodes.name: _commit_if_changed
nodes.summary: Commits the rewritten manifest only when its content actually changed.

nodes.name: _run_apply
nodes.summary: --apply mode: rewrites the manifest to exactly match the SSOT and commits when changed.

nodes.name: _target_config_dirs
nodes.summary: Resolves the account-routed Claude config directory (or dirs) so restores install into the account actually in use rather than defaulting to ~/.claude.

nodes.name: _restore_from
nodes.summary: Installs the marketplaces and plugins listed in a given manifest into a target config dir via `claude plugin` calls.

nodes.name: _local_plugins
nodes.summary: Enumerates the plugins currently installed locally in a config dir for comparison against the manifest.

nodes.name: _prune_pass
nodes.summary: In --sync mode, removes local marketplaces and plugins that are absent from the manifest so the local set exactly matches the SSOT.

nodes.name: _run_for_config_dir
nodes.summary: Runs the restore (and optional prune) pass for a single resolved config directory with CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR injected.

nodes.summary: Index page for the cherry-picked Claude Code plugin guides used in this dotfiles repo, pointing to per-plugin docs and noting that claude/plugin/plugins.json is the SSOT for the full installed list.

nodes.summary: Design spec for a general-purpose git deploy workflow guide extending git-help with deploy, release, rollback, pitfalls, and principles topics, including dispatcher wiring and test plan.

nodes.name: alias_conflict_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check that flags shell aliases whose names collide with existing functions to prevent silent shadowing.

nodes.name: check_alias_function_conflict
nodes.summary: Scans staged shell files for aliases that share a name with a defined function and reports the conflict.

nodes.name: alias_location_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check enforcing that alias definitions live in their designated SSOT location rather than scattered across files.

nodes.name: check_alias_location
nodes.summary: Verifies alias definitions appear only in permitted files, flagging misplaced aliases.

nodes.name: auto_execution_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check detecting top-level code that auto-executes in sourced files, which would run unintended commands on shell startup.

nodes.name: check_auto_execution_in_sourced_files
nodes.summary: Detects auto-executing statements at the top level of sourced shell files that violate the load-time safety guard.

nodes.name: backup_file_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check that blocks accidental commits of editor backup files (e.g. *.bak, *~).

nodes.name: check_backup_files
nodes.summary: Rejects staged files matching backup-file naming patterns to keep the tree clean.

nodes.name: closing_keyword_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check validating that shell control structures are properly closed (fi, done, esac) to catch truncated blocks.

nodes.name: check_closing_keyword
nodes.summary: Ensures shell blocks have their matching closing keywords, flagging unbalanced constructs.

nodes.name: custom_tools_entrypoint_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check ensuring scripts under tools/custom/ are not auto-sourced and must be invoked explicitly per repo convention.

nodes.name: check_auto_executable_in_custom
nodes.summary: Flags tools/custom/ scripts that would be auto-sourced, enforcing explicit-invocation-only policy.

nodes.name: direct_exec_guard_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check verifying sourced files include the required interactive/direct-execution guard clause.

nodes.name: check_direct_exec_guard
nodes.summary: Confirms shell files carry the mandated guard preventing execution when sourced non-interactively.

nodes.name: gh_api_type_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check validating correct type mapping in GitHub API (gh) usage to catch mismatched field types.

nodes.name: check_gh_api_type_mapping
nodes.summary: Inspects gh CLI/API invocations for correct type mappings and flags type mismatches in staged shell files.

nodes.name: hardcoded_home_path_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check that blocks hardcoded absolute home paths, requiring portable $HOME-relative references instead.

nodes.name: check_hardcoded_home_path
nodes.summary: Scans staged files for hardcoded home directory paths and reports non-portable references.

nodes.name: help_integrity_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check verifying help-topic registrations stay consistent with their definitions per the 3-step tool integration pattern.

nodes.name: check_help_integrity
nodes.summary: Validates that help topics are properly registered and their referenced help files exist.

nodes.name: library_purity_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check enforcing that library files only define functions and produce no side effects or top-level output.

nodes.name: check_library_purity
nodes.summary: Ensures library shell files remain side-effect-free, flagging top-level execution or output in pure-library modules.

nodes.name: main_branch_guard.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check that blocks direct commits to the protected main branch.

nodes.name: check_main_branch_guard
nodes.summary: Rejects commits made directly on the main branch to enforce a feature-branch workflow.

nodes.name: naming_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check enforcing snake_case naming conventions for shell functions and reporting violations.

nodes.name: check_function_naming
nodes.summary: Validates that a shell function name conforms to the snake_case convention.

nodes.name: check_naming_violations
nodes.summary: Aggregates function-naming violations across staged shell files and reports them.

nodes.name: pipe_loop_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check flagging the pipe-into-while-loop antipattern that runs the loop body in a subshell and loses variable state.

nodes.name: check_pipe_loop_antipattern
nodes.summary: Detects `cmd | while` subshell antipatterns in staged shell files and reports them.

nodes.name: postmortem_init_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check verifying init/auto-sourcing conventions to prevent postmortem-related initialization regressions.

nodes.name: check_init_auto_sourcing
nodes.summary: Validates auto-sourcing initialization patterns to guard against known postmortem regressions.

nodes.name: shared.sh
nodes.summary: Shared helper library for the pre-commit check scripts, providing a utility to materialize staged-or-worktree file content to a temp path.

nodes.name: write_staged_or_worktree_to_tmp
nodes.summary: Writes a file's staged content (falling back to worktree copy) to a temp path so checks operate on the to-be-committed version.

nodes.name: shebang_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check enforcing the correct shebang line for shell scripts based on file type.

nodes.name: get_expected_shebang
nodes.summary: Returns the expected shebang string for a given shell file according to repo conventions.

nodes.name: check_shebang_violation
nodes.summary: Compares a file's actual shebang against the expected one and reports mismatches.

nodes.name: shellcheck_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check running shellcheck over staged shell files and reporting lint findings.

nodes.name: check_shellcheck
nodes.summary: Invokes shellcheck on staged shell files and surfaces any reported issues as commit blockers.

nodes.name: subshell_sourcing_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check flagging the bash-only `source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/..."` pattern that breaks cross-shell sourcing.

nodes.name: check_subshell_sourcing
nodes.summary: Detects non-portable BASH_SOURCE-relative sourcing that fails under zsh and reports it.

nodes.name: ux_usage_check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-commit check enforcing use of ux_lib output functions instead of raw echo/printf/tput in app scripts.

nodes.name: check_ux_library_usage
nodes.summary: Flags raw echo/printf/tput calls in scripts that should route output through the ux_lib helpers.

nodes.name: check_port_registry.sh
nodes.summary: Validates the PORTS.md port registry: checks that index values are unique integers and that each row's backend/frontend/db ports match the decade-block formula (9200 + index*10).
nodes.languageNotes: POSIX sh (set -eu) so it runs identically under bash and zsh CI.

nodes.name: debug_pip_config.sh
nodes.summary: Diagnostic script that reports pip's effective configuration — environment variables, config file locations, symlinks, proxy state, and runtime version.

nodes.name: disable-git-crypt-local.sh
nodes.summary: Host-side safety net (#594) that force-disables leftover local git-crypt smudge/clean filter config so stale filters can't reintroduce InvalidSymbol/NBSP corruption on checkout; idempotent and always exits 0.

nodes.name: flush-transfer.sh
nodes.summary: Empties the one-shot cross-PC transfer/ directory by removing all its files from git, then committing and pushing the deletion.

nodes.name: lint_docs_filenames.sh
nodes.summary: Lints docs/ filenames against the kebab-case policy (#1027), exempting conventional uppercase files and failing CI only for enforced tiers (docs/adr, docs/requirement) while warning on the rest.
nodes.languageNotes: POSIX sh predicates use case globs rather than regex for exemption/enforcement matching.

nodes.name: setup-skills-ssot.sh
nodes.summary: Links claude/skills/ as the SSOT into OpenCode, Codex, and Gemini CLI skill directories, supporting entry-level compose synthesis and a Codex allowlist to stay within its context budget; invoked by setup.sh.
nodes.languageNotes: Large bash orchestration with multiple per-CLI linking strategies and verification passes.

nodes.name: verify-config-files.sh
nodes.summary: Post-setup integrity check that verifies active symlink targets parse as valid JSON and contain no invisible corruption bytes (NBSP/BOM/NUL); a fail-loud safety net for the #594 corruption symptoms.

nodes.name: read_codex_allowlist
nodes.summary: Reads the .codex-allowlist file into a list of skill names permitted for Codex linking.

nodes.name: codex_skill_is_allowed
nodes.summary: Predicate returning whether a given skill name appears in the Codex allowlist.

nodes.name: collect_codex_homes
nodes.summary: Discovers the set of Codex home directories on the machine that should receive skill links.

nodes.name: link_skills_compose
nodes.summary: Creates the entry-level compose synthesis directory linking each SSOT skill into a target CLI's skills directory (shared across OpenCode/Gemini).

nodes.name: link_skills_individual
nodes.summary: Links each SSOT skill individually into a target CLI skills directory.

nodes.name: link_skills_individual_codex
nodes.summary: Codex-specific linking that preserves the local .system directory, links only allowlisted skills, and prunes non-allowlisted skills to respect Codex's context budget.

nodes.name: verify_link
nodes.summary: Verifies that a created symlink resolves to its expected SSOT target and reports mismatches.

nodes.name: _scan_bytes

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with README.md to understand what this project is: an opinionated, modular Bash/Zsh dotfiles system for reproducible terminal environments across WSL, Linux, and macOS. It lays out the quick-start install, the three-layer shell design, and the key subsystems, giving you the roadmap for the rest of this tour.

tour.title: Setup Orchestrator
tour.description: setup.sh is the true entry point of the project. It runs each module's own setup script (bash, zsh, git, claude), configures environment-specific config, and creates the shell symlinks (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, ~/.gitconfig) that make the dotfiles active. install.sh supplements it with optional IDE and database integration plus a symlink re-run.
tour.languageLesson: These are POSIX-style orchestrator scripts: one top-level script delegates to per-module setup scripts, so each subsystem owns its own install logic and the root script stays a thin coordinator.

tour.title: Shell Entry Points
tour.description: Once symlinks are in place, an interactive shell sources one of these two loaders. bash/main.bash guards against non-interactive shells, detects the Codex environment, and wires up help plus the shared shell-common code; zsh/main.zsh does the zsh equivalent, sourcing shell-common first and then its own app/ and env/ modules. This is where every terminal session actually begins.
tour.languageLesson: Every output-producing file opens with an interactive guard (case $- in *i*) so that non-interactive shells return early. This keeps scripts, cron, and CI from executing interactive-only output.

tour.title: Shared Shell Core
tour.description: Both entry points from Step 3 converge on shell-common, the POSIX-compatible library shared by bash and zsh. shell-common/setup.sh links and generates per-tool configuration (npm, bun, uv, pip, cargo and more), while AGENTS.md documents the directory-placement decision tree that governs what gets auto-sourced versus called explicitly. This is the layer that keeps the two shells in sync.

tour.title: The UX Library
tour.description: Building on the shared core, ux_lib.sh is the mandatory presentation layer: every script must render output through its primitives (ux_header, ux_success, ux_error, ux_info) rather than raw echo/printf/tput. UX_GUIDELINES.md codifies why, so terminal output stays visually consistent across the whole system. Keep this in mind as you read later subsystems.

tour.title: Tool Integrations & Help
tour.description: Tool integrations are the growth surface of the dotfiles: each file under tools/integrations wires one external tool (agy, claude, docker, nvm...) into the shell. my_help.sh is the registry that surfaces per-tool help. Adding a tool follows a fixed 3-file pattern (integration + help + registration), so this pair shows the convention newcomers will most often extend.

tour.title: Git Hook System
tour.description: The git/ module manages a 2-tier hook system. hook-config.sh is the single source of truth for regex patterns, thresholds, and path exclusions; the pre-commit wrapper dispatches to the individual checks under git/hooks/checks/. This is how the repo enforces its own conventions (no emojis, POSIX rules, alias placement) automatically at commit time.

tour.title: Protected-Branch & Leak Guard
tour.description: The other half of the hook system runs at push time. pre-push enforces a protected-branch check plus an upstream leak guard, and pre-push-rules.sh is its SSOT. The leak guard stays inert until you export the upstream-remote and leak-pattern variables, an intentional opt-in so the same dotfiles work safely across personal and work machines.

tour.title: Claude Code Integration
tour.description: The claude/ module integrates the repo with Claude Code. claude/setup.sh symlinks skills and docs into each account's config directory and copies settings.json as a real file (not a symlink) so that /model writes don't dirty the tracked SSOT. AGENTS.md explains the full symlink-vs-copy scheme and the personal-override merge behavior.

tour.title: Installation & Tooling Config
tour.description: mise.toml is the task runner and toolchain manifest that drives the lint/fix/test commands (mise run lint, fix, test) referenced throughout the project, while pyproject.toml configures the Python side (ruff, mypy). Together they define the reproducible developer toolchain that the CI in Step 12 also relies on.
tour.languageLesson: mise.toml declares tasks and pinned tool versions in TOML; a single 'mise run <task>' abstraction lets contributors and CI invoke the exact same lint/test steps without memorizing raw commands.

tour.title: The Test Suite
tour.description: tests/test is the unified runner that orchestrates bats shell unit tests, golden-rules static checks, and pytest integration tests, with parallel and serial modes. It is the practical verification layer for everything covered so far, and the same entry point CI invokes. Run it locally before committing to catch what the hooks in Steps 7-8 also check.
tour.languageLesson: bats is a TAP-producing test framework for shell scripts; pairing it with pytest lets one runner cover both the shell (bats) and Python (pytest) sides of a polyglot repo.

tour.title: Continuous Integration
tour.description: The capstone: ci.yml runs the lint and test jobs on every push and pull request, with concurrency cancellation so superseded runs stop early. It ties the whole tour together — the setup flow, the shell layers, the git hooks, and the test runner from Step 11 all get exercised here to keep the dotfiles reproducible for everyone.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions YAML uses 'on' triggers to fire workflows and a concurrency group to auto-cancel outdated runs, so only the latest commit on a branch consumes CI minutes.
